items.title: India Ranks 87th in Global Energy Transition Index 2026
items.whyInNews: The World Economic Forum released the Global Energy Transition Index 2026, where India ranked 87th out of 115 countries. The index measures countries' performance in energy transition.
items.summary: The Global Energy Transition Index 2026 report was released by the World Economic Forum on 16 April 2026. Sweden ranked first in the index, followed by Norway and Denmark. India's rank improved by 4 positions from the previous year.
keyPoints: India ranked 87th out of 115 countries
Sweden ranked first in the index
India's rank improved by 4 positions from the previous year
importantPoints: Global Energy Transition Index was first released in 2018
The index measures countries' performance in energy transition
World Economic Forum is headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland
The index is released annually
India aims to achieve 50% of its energy from non-fossil fuels by 2030
items.examRelevance: This topic is relevant for SSC CGL and CHSL exams under the economy section, as it deals with India's energy transition and global rankings.

items.title: NASA's Artemis Program to Send First Woman to Moon by 2027
items.whyInNews: NASA announced that its Artemis program will send the first woman to the moon by 2027. The program aims to establish a sustainable human presence on the lunar surface.
items.summary: The Artemis program was launched by NASA in 2019, with the goal of sending the first woman and the next man to the moon by 2024. However, the deadline has been pushed to 2027 due to technical issues and funding constraints.
keyPoints: NASA's Artemis program to send first woman to moon by 2027
The program aims to establish a sustainable human presence on the lunar surface
The deadline was pushed from 2024 to 2027 due to technical issues and funding constraints
importantPoints: NASA was established in 1958
The Artemis program was launched in 2019
The program is named after the Greek goddess of the hunt, Artemis
The program aims to establish a sustainable human presence on the lunar surface
The first woman to walk in space was Valentina Tereshkova in 1963
items.examRelevance: This topic is relevant for SSC CGL and CHSL exams under the science section, as it deals with space exploration and NASA's programs.

items.title: India to Host G20 Summit in New Delhi on 9-10 September 2026
items.whyInNews: India will host the G20 Summit in New Delhi on 9-10 September 2026. The summit will bring together leaders from the world's 20 largest economies to discuss global issues.
items.summary: The G20 Summit will be held at the Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi, and will be attended by leaders from the G20 member countries, as well as invited guest countries. The summit will focus on issues such as global economic growth, climate change, and sustainable development.
keyPoints: India to host G20 Summit in New Delhi on 9-10 September 2026
The summit will bring together leaders from the world's 20 largest economies
The summit will focus on issues such as global economic growth, climate change, and sustainable development
importantPoints: G20 was established in 1999
The G20 Summit is held annually
India is the current chair of the G20
The summit will be attended by leaders from the G20 member countries, as well as invited guest countries
The G20 member countries account for 85% of global GDP
items.examRelevance: This topic is relevant for SSC CGL and CHSL exams under the international relations section, as it deals with global summits and international cooperation.

items.title: Indian Cricketer Virat Kohli Becomes Fastest to Reach 13,000 Runs in International Cricket
items.whyInNews: Indian cricketer Virat Kohli became the fastest player to reach 13,000 runs in international cricket, achieving the feat in 463 matches.
items.summary: Virat Kohli achieved the feat during the second ODI against Australia on 17 April 2026. He surpassed the record held by Sachin Tendulkar, who took 474 matches to reach 13,000 runs.
keyPoints: Virat Kohli became the fastest player to reach 13,000 runs in international cricket
He achieved the feat in 463 matches
He surpassed the record held by Sachin Tendulkar
importantPoints: Virat Kohli made his international debut in 2008
He has played 463 matches and scored 13,000 runs
Sachin Tendulkar held the previous record, taking 474 matches to reach 13,000 runs
Virat Kohli is the captain of the Indian cricket team
He has won numerous awards, including the ICC Cricketer of the Year award
items.examRelevance: This topic is relevant for SSC CGL and CHSL exams under the sports section, as it deals with cricket and Indian sports personalities.

items.title: Government Launches Scheme to Promote Electric Vehicles in India
items.whyInNews: The government launched a scheme to promote electric vehicles in India, with a budget of Rs 10,000 crore. The scheme aims to encourage the adoption of electric vehicles and reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
items.summary: The scheme was launched by the Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises on 15 April 2026. It will provide incentives to manufacturers and buyers of electric vehicles, as well as support for the development of charging infrastructure.
keyPoints: Government launched scheme to promote electric vehicles in India
The scheme has a budget of Rs 10,000 crore
The scheme aims to encourage the adoption of electric vehicles and reduce greenhouse gas emissions
importantPoints: The scheme was launched by the Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises
The scheme will provide incentives to manufacturers and buyers of electric vehicles
The scheme will support the development of charging infrastructure
India aims to have 30% of its vehicles electric by 2030
The scheme is part of the government's efforts to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and promote sustainable development
items.examRelevance: This topic is relevant for SSC CGL and CHSL exams under the economy section, as it deals with government schemes and initiatives to promote electric vehicles.

items.title: India Ranks 40th in Global Innovation Index 2026
items.whyInNews: India ranked 40th in the Global Innovation Index 2026, released by the World Intellectual Property Organization. The index measures countries' innovation performance.
items.summary: The Global Innovation Index 2026 report was released on 14 April 2026. Switzerland ranked first in the index, followed by the United States and Sweden. India's rank improved by 3 positions from the previous year.
keyPoints: India ranked 40th in the Global Innovation Index 2026
The index measures countries' innovation performance
Switzerland ranked first in the index
importantPoints: Global Innovation Index was first released in 2007
The index is released annually by the World Intellectual Property Organization
India's rank improved by 3 positions from the previous year
The index measures countries' innovation performance across 81 indicators
India aims to become a global innovation hub by 2030
items.examRelevance: This topic is relevant for SSC CGL and CHSL exams under the science section, as it deals with innovation and intellectual property.

items.title: Indian Author Geetanjali Shree Wins International Booker Prize 2026
items.whyInNews: Indian author Geetanjali Shree won the International Booker Prize 2026 for her novel 'Tomb of Sand'. The prize is awarded to the best translated novel of the year.
items.summary: The International Booker Prize 2026 was announced on 13 April 2026. Geetanjali Shree's novel 'Tomb of Sand' was translated by Daisy Rockwell and published by Tilted Axis Press.
keyPoints: Geetanjali Shree won the International Booker Prize 2026
The prize is awarded to the best translated novel of the year
Geetanjali Shree's novel 'Tomb of Sand' was translated by Daisy Rockwell
importantPoints: Geetanjali Shree is an Indian author and translator
The International Booker Prize was established in 2005
The prize is awarded annually to the best translated novel of the year
Geetanjali Shree's novel 'Tomb of Sand' is her first novel to be translated into English
The prize is considered one of the most prestigious literary awards in the world
items.examRelevance: This topic is relevant for SSC CGL and CHSL exams under the awards section, as it deals with literary awards and Indian authors.
